title: Extract HTTP transport from openai/mod.rs to openai/http.rs
purpose: Create structural symmetry for the dual-transport architecture by extracting the HTTP+SSE transport code into its own file. This is a pure refactor with zero behavioral change — it moves the SenderProvider struct and its execute() method from openai/mod.rs to openai/http.rs, establishing the pattern where each transport is a self-contained module alongside the shared OpenAI API code.
task: Move the SenderProvider struct and its execute() method (~220 lines) from openai/mod.rs to a new openai/http.rs file. Update mod.rs to declare the new module and use its public interface. Verify that all existing tests pass unchanged — the extraction must be a pure refactor with identical behavior.

requirements.title: Create openai/http.rs with SenderProvider
requirements.spec: The SenderProvider struct and its execute() method SHALL be defined in openai/http.rs. The struct SHALL be pub(super) so that mod.rs can construct it. The execute method signature SHALL remain unchanged.
acceptance: execute() method has identical signature to current mod.rs version

requirements.title: Update mod.rs module declarations
requirements.spec: openai/mod.rs SHALL declare mod http and use SenderProvider from it in the Provider::stream() spawned task. The imports previously used by SenderProvider SHALL move to http.rs.
acceptance: mod.rs contains pub(super) mod http or mod http declaration
No unused imports remain in mod.rs after extraction

requirements.title: Behavioral parity verification
requirements.spec: All existing tests in openai/mod.rs test modules SHALL pass unchanged after extraction. The ProviderEvent stream produced by the HTTP transport SHALL be identical to the pre-extraction behavior.
acceptance: All tests in openai/mod.rs pass without modification
No behavioral difference observable via DebugDumper output

boundaries: SHALL NOT change any behavior — pure refactor only
SHALL NOT modify the Provider trait or any public API
SHALL NOT move test modules — tests stay in mod.rs
SHALL NOT add WebSocket code — that is PW-004+ scope
verification: SenderProvider no longer defined in openai/mod.rs (grep confirms)
openai/http.rs exists and contains SenderProvider + execute()
